pattern: A cyclic distribution where each handout increases by one until candies run out, teaching careful loop termination on the final partial gift.
hints: The amount given grows by exactly one every step, regardless of which person receives it.
Keep a running 'give' counter and a remaining-candies counter; the recipient cycles modulo the number of people.
Loop while candies remain, hand the smaller of the next intended amount and what is left, subtract it, and advance the give counter and the cyclic index.

edge_inputs.description: first example, last person gets a partial gift
candies run out exactly at a full round
more people than candies leaves trailing zeros

stress.description: random candy totals and row sizes covering many wrap-arounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    candies = rng.randrange(1, 200000)
    num_people = rng.randrange(1, 1001)
    return [candies, num_people]
stress.note: size is ignored; bounds keep the simulation fast

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def distributeCandies(self, candies, num_people):
        ans = [0] * num_people
        give = 1
        i = 0
        while candies > 0:
            take = give if give <= candies else candies
            ans[i % num_people] += take
            candies -= take
            give += 1
            i += 1
        return ans

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def distributeCandies(self, candies, num_people):
        ans = [0] * num_people
        give = 1
        idx = 0
        while candies > 0:
            take = min(give, candies)
            ans[idx] += take
            candies -= take
            give += 1
            idx += 1
            if idx == num_people:
                idx = 0
        return ans
